sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: The Beneficiary has frequently served as a judge for the works of others in the field of communication and market research. Details can be found in Exhibits C.

sections.heading: 1. The Beneficiary's Appointment as the Deputy Director of the 13th Tiger Roar Awards [Exhibit C]
sections.content:
The Beneficiary was formally appointed as the Deputy Director of the 13th Tiger Roar Awards Organizing Committee, as certified in an official appointment letter. [Exhibit X - x] The appointment letter, which bears the seals of both the China Advertising Association of Commerce (registration number 1100000195568) and the Tiger Roar Awards Organizing Committee, confirms: "This is to certify that you are appointed as the Deputy Director of the 13th Tiger Roar Awards Organizing Committee." [Exhibit X - x]

As Deputy Director, the Beneficiary held a senior leadership position within the organizing body that oversaw the evaluation and selection of winners from thousands of entries across the digital marketing industry. [Exhibit X - x]

Professor Liping Sun, a professor at the Department of Sociology at Tsinghua University and PhD supervisor, has endorsed the Beneficiary's qualifications and judging expertise in the fields of communication and market research. [Exhibit X - x] Professor Sun is a distinguished sociologist who "mainly engages in research on social modernization, changes in social structure, oral social history, and sociology of transition societies" and has "published more than 10 monographs (including co-authored works) and dozens of papers," lending additional authority to his endorsement of the Beneficiary's judging contributions. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: 2. The Beneficiary's Appointment as the Judge of the First China Digital Marketing Conference [Exhibit C]
sections.content:
In January 2016, the Beneficiary was invited to serve as a "Reviewing Judge" for the First China Digital Marketing Conference, held at the Beijing National Convention Center. [Exhibit X - x] The conference was organized by the Digital Marketing Committee of China ("DMCC"), operating under the auspices of the Chinese Ministry of Commerce. [Exhibit X - x]

As documented in email correspondence from the DMCC Secretariat, the Beneficiary's judging responsibilities encompassed voting across eight categories of award candidates, and his "voting power account[ed] for 80%, which will directly determine the final award winners." [Exhibit X - x] The DMCC instructed the reviewing judges to vote "according to your professional experience" among the candidates from January 4th to January 7th. [Exhibit X - x]

Following the Beneficiary's evaluation, the DMCC Secretariat acknowledged: "Thank you for Professor Liu's valuable suggestions, we will tabulate the review results within next week." [Exhibit X - x] Media coverage confirms that the conference attracted internet giants such as Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, and Google, along with "hundreds of domestic and foreign brand owners and innovative digital marketing companies," underscoring the significance of the judging role the Beneficiary performed. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: Therefore, the Beneficiary's experience as Deputy Director of the 13th Tiger Roar Awards and as reviewing judge for the First China Digital Marketing Conference not only shows that he has met 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(iv), but also demonstrates that he is among the small percentage of top professionals within the field of communication and market research.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Dr. Liu has frequently served as a judge for the works of others in the field of sociology. Details can be found in Exhibits C.

sections.heading: 1. Dr. Liu's Appointment as the Deputy Director of the 13th Tiger Roar Awards [Exhibit C]
sections.content:
Dr. Liu served as the Deputy Director of the 13th Tiger Roar Awards Organizing Committee. The 13th Tiger Roar Awards were hosted by the China Advertising Association of Commerce (CAAC). [Exhibit X - x] Founded in 1981, the China Advertising Association of Commerce was the first national advertising industry association in China and continues to contribute to the high-quality development and construction of China's advertising industry. [Exhibit X - x]

According to several reputable media outlets, including Sohu and China Daily, as a definitive and forward-looking recognition in the field of brand marketing digitalization and digital business communication, the Tiger Roar Awards set the highest standard in the industry. They concentrate on the comprehensive strategies employed by enterprises for implementing digital marketing communication, which includes the use of data, media, and a variety of technological methods. [Exhibit X - x] As reported by PR Newswire, the awards recognized and celebrated outstanding contributions from a diverse range of sectors, including brand owners, agencies, media advertising, technology companies, and other organizations. For instance, one of the awarded projects was from the ZEISS Group, a global leader in the optics and photo electricity industry. This illustrates the high caliber of works judged under Dr. Liu's stewardship. [Exhibit X - x]

As the Deputy Director of the Awards Organizing Committee, Dr. Liu was involved in overseeing and administering a complex judging process. The process involved an online review by over 650 public jury members, a panel discussion and final review by more than 90 final judging panel members, and reexamination by more than 10 executive members. Further adding to the magnitude of his involvement, the 13th Tiger Roar Awards received more than 4,000 case works from over 800 participating companies, showing the extent of the judgment process and the depth of expertise required to assess these submissions. Dr. Liu's position as Deputy Director and his key role in this rigorous review process demonstrate his significant capacity and trusted authority to judge others' work in his field. [Exhibit X - x] In addition, Dr. Liping Sun, another renowned scholar in the field of sociology, also participated in the Tiger Roar Awards selection with Dr. Liu. Dr. Sun is a professor and doctoral supervisor in the Department of Sociology at Tsinghua University, and has engaged in a teaching and research career in the field of sociology for more than twenty years. [Exhibit X - x]

: Sohu.com Limited (NASDAQ: SOHU), is a mainstream media platform in China.
: China Daily: Established in 1981, China Daily is China's leading English-language newspaper. With a combined print, online and mobile readership of more than 350 million, it serves a vital role in telling the world about China. [Exhibit X - x]
: PR Newswire, is a leading global provider of news distribution and earned media software and services. PR Newswire serves tens of thousands of clients from offices in the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and the Asia-Pacific regions.

sections.heading: 2. Dr. Liu's Appointment as the Judge of the First China Digital Marketing Conference [Exhibit C]
sections.content:
In January 2022, Dr. Liu served as a judge at the First China Digital Marketing Conference. [Exhibit X - x] This landmark event was hosted by the Digital Marketing Committee (DMCC) of the China Advertising Association of Commerce, under the auspices of the Chinese Ministry of Commerce, and took place at the Beijing National Convention Center from January 21 to January 22, 2016. [Exhibit X - x]

The DMCC is a non-profit organization founded by notable Internet technology enterprises, digital marketing service enterprises, data technology research and service institutions, digital media and other entities, along with industry experts in China. Members include globally renowned technology enterprises, large platform group companies, industrial internet ecological companies, big data technology companies, and mobile internet companies, among others. [Exhibit X - x]

The Conference aimed to assist Chinese brands in building digital transformation and upgrading, thereby promoting the development of the entire digital marketing industry chain ecosystem. The conference's theme, "Building the Right Path in the Intelligent Digital Era," invited comprehensive discussions on digital marketing in the era of Internet+ from a global perspective. Key participants included industry giants such as Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, Google, NetEase, Sohu, Sina, Acxiom, and Criteo, as well as hundreds of domestic and foreign brand owners and innovative digital marketing companies. [Exhibit X - x]

Dr. Liu's role as a judge at the conference was based on his exceptional knowledge in sociology, particularly in understanding societal behavior in the digital age. His expertise provided critical insights into assessing digital marketing strategies and their societal impacts. According to a letter from DMCC, Dr. Liu's judgement carried a weightage of 80%, directly influencing the final list of award winners. This further underscores his significant role and recognition in the field related to his professional expertise. [Exhibit X - x]

Therefore, Dr. Liu's experience in participating in the 13th Tiger Roar Awards and the First China Digital Marketing Conference not only shows that he has met 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3)(iv), but also shows that he is among the small percentage of top professionals within the field of sociology in China.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Pursuant to 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(iv), the submitted exhibits establish that the Beneficiary has participated as a judge of the work of others in the same or an allied field of specialization.

sections.heading: Judging Role and Conference Background
sections.content:
The record shows that the Beneficiary served as a judge for the First China Digital Marketing Conference & China Digital Marketing Commendation Conference in Beijing in January 2016 [Exhibit X - x]. The judging credential specifically identifies him as "Judge Dehuan Liu" for that conference [Exhibit X - x]. The event was organized through the Digital Marketing Committee of China, an arm of the China Advertising Association of Commerce [Exhibit X - x]. The China Advertising Association of Commerce was founded in 1981 and is described in the exhibits as the first national association of the advertising industry in China [Exhibit X - x]. The Digital Marketing Committee itself was established in Beijing on August 14, 2015 as a nonprofit organization focused on the digital marketing field and composed of prominent enterprises, research institutions, media entities, and industry experts [Exhibit X - x].

The conference was hosted by that Committee under the auspices of the Chinese Ministry of Commerce, was held at the Beijing National Convention Center from January 21 to 22, 2016, and was designed to promote digital transformation and upgrading across the industry [Exhibit X - x]. The exhibits further show that the conference attracted major Internet and technology companies, including Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, Google, NetEase, Sohu, Sina, Acxiom, and Criteo, along with hundreds of domestic and foreign brand owners and innovative digital marketing companies [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Formalized Appointment and Evaluative Authority
sections.content: The Beneficiary's appointment as a judge was not incidental, but formalized through documentary evidence showing that he was selected to serve in a reviewing capacity for this national industry event [Exhibit X - x]. His judging certificate was issued in connection with the First China Digital Marketing Conference & China Digital Marketing Commendation Conference and identifies his role by title rather than merely as an attendee or participant [Exhibit X - x]. The contemporaneous email correspondence from the DMCC Secretariat was addressed directly to the Beneficiary at his personal email address and referred to him as one of the "reviewing judges," confirming that he had been invited to evaluate candidate entries rather than perform a ceremonial function [Exhibit X - x]. That same correspondence thanked "Professor Liu" for his "valuable suggestions," demonstrating that the organizers solicited and respected his substantive evaluative input [Exhibit X - x]. The selection of the Beneficiary for this role is consistent with the stature of the host organization, which the exhibits describe as a leading industry body bringing together enterprises, service institutions, media organizations, and experts in digital marketing [Exhibit X - x]. In short, the record supports that the Beneficiary was chosen because of recognized professional expertise relevant to judging digital marketing work [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Scope and Rigor of Judging Responsibilities
sections.content: The scope and rigor of the Beneficiary's judging responsibilities are also documented in the exhibits [Exhibit X - x]. The conference established eight major awards to recognize innovation by brand owners, digital companies, media institutions, and practitioners [Exhibit X - x]. The DMCC Secretariat instructed the reviewing judges to vote from January 4 through January 7, 2016 among candidates in all eight award categories according to the stated requirements [Exhibit X - x]. The same communication explained that the judges' voting power accounted for 80% and would directly determine the final award winners, showing that the Beneficiary's evaluation carried decisive weight in the outcome of the competition [Exhibit X - x]. The organizers also circulated submission data reflecting a structured review process across multiple categories, including main brand, digital enterprises, digital marketing product effectiveness, personality, and media categories [Exhibit X - x]. By assigning the Beneficiary direct voting authority over a multi-category awards process at a nationally organized digital marketing conference, the organization entrusted him with judging the professional work of others in a meaningful and outcome-determinative manner [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Significance of the Judging Service
sections.content: With respect to fellow judges, the submitted exhibits for this particular judging role do not identify by name the other reviewing judges who served alongside the Beneficiary [Exhibit X - x]. Nevertheless, the record still demonstrates the significance of the Beneficiary's service because the conference drew participation from major technology companies and leading market actors from across the digital economy [Exhibit X - x]. The event was positioned as an industry-wide platform for brand transformation, digital upgrading, and recognition of innovation, which underscores the professional importance of the work being judged [Exhibit X - x]. The fact that the judging panel's vote accounted for 80% of the final result further shows that the panel's determinations had concrete impact on which entrants would receive formal commendation [Exhibit X - x]. Thus, even though the co-judges are not individually named in these exhibits, the documentary record still establishes that the Beneficiary served in a serious adjudicative role within a substantial and nationally oriented professional forum [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: Accordingly, the submitted evidence demonstrates the Beneficiary's participation as a judge of the work of others in the same or an allied field of specialization under 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(iv).
